questions.question: In digital logic design, what is the primary function of a multiplexer (MUX)?
options: To store a single bit of data
To amplify an input signal
To select one of many input signals and route it to a single output
To combine multiple input signals into a single output
To generate a clock signal

questions.question: In cache memory management, what is the primary role of a 'dirty bit' associated with a cache line?
options: Specifies the cache line's access permissions.
Denotes the priority of the cache line for eviction.
Signifies that the cache line contains an error.
Indicates if the data in the cache line has been modified but not yet written back to main memory.
Marks the cache line as invalid and ready for replacement.

questions.question: In the context of Design for Testability (DFT) for digital integrated circuits, what is the primary function of 'scan chains'?
options: To accelerate the logic synthesis process by optimizing critical paths.
To convert all sequential elements into a shift register during test mode for easy observation and control.
To dynamically adjust the clock frequency based on system load.
To verify the functional correctness of the design under normal operating conditions.
To reduce static power consumption during standby mode.

questions.question: In multi-core processor architectures, what is the primary purpose and characteristic of an 'atomic operation'?
options: An operation that accesses data directly from main memory, bypassing all cache levels.
An operation primarily used for debugging purposes to halt processor execution.
An operation that uses specialized hardware to perform floating-point calculations at high speed.
An operation that is guaranteed to complete entirely without interruption by any other process or thread, or not at all.
An operation that allows multiple instructions to execute simultaneously on a single core.

questions.question: In digital IC design flow, what does 'RTL' stand for, and what kind of design description does it primarily represent?
options: Routing and Timing Layout, a physical design stage focused on interconnects.
Real-Time Logic, indicating synchronous operations with strict timing constraints.
Reduced Transaction Language, an abstract modeling language for system-level simulation.
Recursive Test Logic, a methodology for automated test pattern generation.
Register-Transfer Level, describing the circuit's behavior in terms of data flow between registers and combinational logic.
